name: Chocolate-Walnut Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Toast walnut halves on a cookie sheet for about 10 minutes until golden, then let cool and chop coarsely.
2. In a double boiler, melt chocolate and butter together. Remove from heat and stir until smooth. Let cool for 10 minutes.
3. Sift flour and baking powder into a bowl.
4. In a large bowl, beat eggs lightly. Gradually add sugar, then orange zest. Stir in the cooled chocolate mixture until blended. Stir in the sifted dry ingredients until incorporated. Fold in chopped walnuts. Divide dough in half and wrap each portion in plastic wrap; refrigerate at least 1 hour or overnight.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F and grease a large baking sheet with butter. Shape each portion of dough into a log measuring 14 inches long by 2-1/2 inches wide. Place logs 4 inches apart on the prepared sheet, smoothing tops and sides with a rubber spatula.
6. Bake for 40-45 minutes or until firm when pressed in the center. Remove from oven; do not turn off the heat.
7. Transfer logs to a cutting board and cut each into 1/2-inch slices. Stand slices upright on the sheet. Return to oven and bake for an additional 15 minutes, or until crisp. Cool completely on wire racks.
